source_text:
Chennai Residents Raise Concern Over Damaged Roads After Rain

Chennai, July 3: Residents in several parts of Chennai have raised concerns over damaged roads and slow drainage repair work after repeated spells of rain exposed weak civic infrastructure in key neighbourhoods.

Local residents said several interior roads have developed potholes, making daily travel difficult for motorists, school vans and elderly pedestrians. In some areas, stagnant water has remained for several days as stormwater drains are either blocked or incomplete.

Civic officials said repair work has started in priority zones and additional teams have been deployed to inspect low-lying streets. They added that temporary patchwork will be completed first, followed by permanent road relaying once the rain reduces.

Residents, however, said the work must be monitored more closely. They complained that the same stretches are repaired repeatedly every year without a permanent solution. Shopkeepers in affected areas said poor road conditions have reduced customer footfall and increased vehicle damage.

Urban planning experts said road repair, drain desilting and utility work should be coordinated before the monsoon season instead of being handled as separate emergency tasks.

The civic body has asked residents to report damaged stretches through the official complaint helpline and mobile application. Officials said complaints related to waterlogging and damaged roads will be reviewed on a daily basis until the situation improves.
